_help: Declared construct-classes the project has ruled NO-COUNTERPART for the ngspice v26->v41 port. This file is the STRUCTURAL BACKSTOP referenced by build-ledger.mjs --check and the port-loop driver preflight: a ruled construct that is still workable-PENDING (mapped tsFunction + not recon-blocked) in the ledger is a FROZEN-CONSTRUCT LEAK and FAILS --check. The guard only DETECTS and FAILS LOUDLY; it never auto-writes a NO-COUNTERPART ruling. The fix for a leak is to write the construct's NO-COUNTERPART ruling into the device's planning/<dev>-decisions.json overlay (or split the hunk so only the frozen arm is NO-COUNTERPART), then rebuild the ledger. Each entry: id (stable construct-class id), description (what the construct is), rationale (why it has no digiTS counterpart, drawn from the ruled NO-COUNTERPART rationales / ESCALATIONS.md), match.tokens (substrings; a hunk matches this construct ONLY when a token appears on an ADDED or changed diff line of the hunk body -- '+'/'-' lines, never surrounding context -- so a portable hunk that merely mentions a token in context is NOT flagged).

constructs.description: The trnoise_state / trrandom_state noise-source lifecycle subsystem: trnoise_state_init/_get/_free, the f_alpha 1/f synthesizer, the RTS (random-telegraph-signal) trap state machine (RTScapTime/RTSemTime/RTSCAPT/RTSEMT), and trrandom_state_get value refresh. Drives the TRNOISE/TRRANDOM source-waveform arms of VSRCaccept/VSRCload, ISRCaccept/ISRCload, and the ISRCparam/VSRCparam alter-free calls.
constructs.rationale: Out of scope for a browser digital-logic simulator (user ruling 2026-06-02, ruled 3x; TRNOISE re-escalated 4x before the backstop). maths-misc#recon/randnumb delivered only SeededRng primitives (CombLCGTaus/gauss/poisson on monte-carlo.ts), NOT the trnoise_state/trrandom_state lifecycle. The TRNOISE/TRRANDOM source-waveform arms have no digiTS counterpart and are not reconstructed under vsrc#recon/waveformModel or isrc#recon/coeffWaveforms. NOTE on tokens: the bare words `TRNOISE`/`TRRANDOM` are NOT tokens because in the diff they appear only on unchanged `case TRNOISE:` context lines; the construct's ADDED lines carry the RTS shot-noise state-machine identifiers (RTScapTime/RTSemTime/RTSCAPT/RTSEMT/exprand) and the trnoise_state/trrandom_state lifecycle calls, which is what an applier would have to reproduce in TS.

constructs.description: The newcompat.xs foreign-SPICE compatibility-mode branches in the PULSE arms of ISRCaccept/ISRCload and VSRCload: phase-normalization (phase=fmod(phase/360,1); deltat=phase*PER; time+=deltat) and the !newcompat.xs time>tmax guard, reading the global newcompat compatmode.h struct.
constructs.rationale: digiTS has no newcompat/compat-mode flag and no compatmode.h include layer; it emulates no foreign-SPICE deck dialect. The newcompat.xs arms were carved out by Phase-0 ledger splits into frozen NO-COUNTERPART sub-items (vsrcload.c#h004, isrcacct.c#h004b, isrcload.c#h004b); the portable PULSE remainder (PHASE>0 cap + CKTsetBreak chain) is a separate workable sub-item.

constructs.description: The SPfrontEnd device-warning / device-info diagnostic channel: ERR_INFO / ERR_WARNING device messages routed through IFerrorf (e.g. VSRCtemp's 'DC 0 assumed' and 'dc value used for op instead of transient time=0 value' ERR_INFO emissions).
constructs.rationale: Front-end diagnostic with no numerical effect; digiTS has no SPfrontEnd / IFerrorf device-warning channel. Ruled NO-COUNTERPART (vsrctemp.c#h002b split sub-item) -- it is a device-warning emission, not a behavioral computation.

constructs.description: The #ifdef RFSPICE S-parameter / port-network analysis blocks: VSRCresNode/CKTportCount/port TSTALLOCs, VSRCisPort/portZ0/portFreq/portPhase param-application, the RFSPICE acLoad/load g0-admittance port-stamp branches, and the VSRC_PORTNUM/PORTZ0/PORTPWR/PORTFREQ/PORTPHASE ask/param cases.
constructs.rationale: S-parameter / RF port-network analysis is not implemented (analysis-scope.md, ratified). digiTS has no RFSPICE build and no port-network surface; every RFSPICE-guarded block has no digiTS counterpart.

constructs.description: The #ifdef XSPICE code-model / event-driven subsystem: XSPICE event includes (evt.h/enh.h), XSPICE-guarded struct members, and the DOING_SP / event-node plumbing.
constructs.rationale: digiTS has no XSPICE code-model / event-driven subsystem and no C include layer mirroring it. Every XSPICE-guarded construct has no digiTS counterpart (acan.c#h001 XSPICE event-include escalation; isrcdefs.h XSPICE member unguard).

constructs.description: The LTRA lossy-transmission-line device model and its DCtran/timestep integration hooks (del_before / LTRA-specific breakpoint and history machinery).
constructs.rationale: The LTRA lossy-transmission-line device is out of the frozen v41 device-class scope (device-class-scope.md). digiTS has no LTRA model; its integration hooks have no counterpart.

constructs.description: The C TSKtask / CKTnewTask / CKTdoJob task-and-job option machinery: TSKtask struct allocation + defaults/copy (CKTnewTask), the task->ckt option copy (CKTdoJob), and the CKTsetOpt switch + OPTtbl IFparm option table.
constructs.rationale: digiTS has no CKTcircuit/TSKtask struct, no CKTnewTask/CKTdoJob method, and no integer-keyed OPTtbl option table; task config (xmu/indVerbosity/epsmin/cshunt) is reimplemented as typed fields on CKTCircuitContext read from ResolvedSimulationParams. The task/job machinery is a C-struct declaration layer with no line-isomorphic TS counterpart.
